title: Turkey Meatball & Zucchini Skillet
description: A delicious and healthy one-pan meal featuring tender turkey meatballs and fresh zucchini, perfect for a quick family dinner. Kid-friendly and easy to prepare!
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground turkey, breadcrumbs, egg, 1/2 tsp Italian seasoning, 1/4 tsp salt, and 1/8 tsp black pepper. Mix gently until just combined, then form into 1-inch meatballs.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add meatballs and cook, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides. Remove meatballs from skillet and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp olive oil to the skillet. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-5 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, remaining 1/2 tsp Italian seasoning, 1/4 tsp salt, and 1/8 tsp black pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Return meatballs to the skillet. Add sliced zucchini. Cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until zucchini is tender and meatballs are cooked through.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with shredded Parmesan cheese.
tips: For extra flavor, you can add a pinch of red pepper flakes to the sauce.
If your kids are picky about zucchini, you can grate it and mix it directly into the meatballs or finely dice it so it blends into the sauce more.
Serve this skillet meal over pasta, rice, or with a side of crusty bread for a complete meal.
